title: agentfront ships as an importable runtime: add it as a dependency, point it at your markdown and your functions, and you get an agent-first MCP server and a discoverable markdown HTTP doc-site — configurable, batteries-included, no hand-rolled surfaces

claims.text: agentfront ships as an importable runtime: add it as a dependency, point it at your markdown and your functions, and you get an agent-first MCP server and a discoverable markdown HTTP doc-site — configurable, batteries-included, no hand-rolled surfaces

honesty_conditions.text: The shipped library delivers the full round-trip: import -> small config -> both an MCP server and a markdown+sitemap HTTP site go live, demonstrated by agentfront serving its own docs/ and MCP this way

claims.text: Two-sided: PRODUCERS are Python tool authors (AgentCulture-org first, anyone second) who want to expose their tool to agents; CONSUMERS are the AI agents that hit the resulting MCP server and markdown HTTP site

honesty_conditions.text: Both sides are real: a PRODUCER stands up the surfaces from import, AND a CONSUMING agent discovers+uses them with only a fetch tool / MCP client — neither side needs bespoke glue

claims.text: A tool author adds 'agentfront' as a dependency, writes a small config (which markdown to serve, which functions to expose as MCP tools), and gets a running MCP server + markdown+sitemap HTTP site — neither surface hand-rolled, both agent-first by construction

honesty_conditions.text: The 'small config' is genuinely small: agentfront's own setup fits in well under ~30 lines and writes zero server/protocol code

claims.text: Today agentfront is a build-time scaffolder: it emits reference trees into .agentfront/ for the author to own and adapt (cite-don't-import). MCP and HTTP surfaces are 'not implemented yet (planned v0.4/v0.5)'. Authors wanting MCP/HTTP today build them by hand, inconsistently; agent-first discipline is only AUDITED after the fact via cli doctor + rubric

honesty_conditions.text: The described 'today' is accurate at pivot time: MCP/HTTP are unimplemented and cli cite/doctor are the only shipped surface — verifiable against the current README and code

hard_questions.text: What is the fate of the SHIPPED surface — 'cli cite' scaffolder, the manifest->three-surfaces vision, and the rubric/doctor? Retired, coexisting, or reframed (e.g. doctor audits the served surfaces instead of emitted code)?

claims.text: Importable runtime EXECUTES the agent-first discipline instead of only auditing it — the surfaces cannot drift from best practice because agentfront IS the surface, not a one-time code emission. It also lowers the bar: configure once, don't scaffold-and-maintain forever

honesty_conditions.text: Drift is structurally impossible, not merely discouraged: there is no code path by which any surface can present a doc/tool the config did not declare

claims.text: Not a general web framework: the HTTP surface serves markdown pages + a sitemap (and maybe a /llms.txt), not arbitrary HTML, templating, or a SPA

honesty_conditions.text: The HTTP output is markdown + sitemap (+ optional llms.txt) only: no HTML templating engine, no asset pipeline, no client-side JS in what is served

claims.text: Not a maximal MCP exposer: the menu stays opinionated-minimal; growing past a threshold warns, mirroring today's rubric principle

honesty_conditions.text: Menu minimalism is enforced in code: exceeding the opinionated tool-count threshold raises a doctor warning, not silent acceptance

claims.text: agentfront dogfoods itself: agentfront's own MCP server + HTTP doc-site are served BY agentfront-the-library from its own docs/. And a third package can 'import agentfront' + write a small config and serve both surfaces with no bespoke server code

honesty_conditions.text: agentfront serves its OWN three surfaces from its own config in CI, AND a from-scratch third package reproduces it via a documented quickstart — both are tested

claims.text: Not authentication/multi-tenant hosting/CDN concerns — it runs a local/process-level server you can put behind your own proxy; deployment is the host's problem

agentfront becomes a RUNTIME FRAMEWORK you import (like FastAPI for agent-first surfaces), not a generator you cite. Resolution of the org tension: 'cite, don't import' governs PATTERNS/SKILLS/reference code you own and modify; a runtime framework is a legitimate dependency (you import Flask, you don't cite it)

hard_questions.text: contradiction with Directly contradicts the repo's standing 'cite, don't import' policy (CLAUDE.md, docs/agent-first.md, docs/skill-sources.md). The entire current README + agent-first doc are built on the generator/citation model?
Is a runtime framework a SANCTIONED exception to 'cite, don't import', or does this pivot rewrite the org policy itself? Until decided, the foundational framing of the repo is in flux

claims.text: The configuration entry point is a Python API: 'import agentfront; app = agentfront.App(...)' with markdown source(s) and tool functions registered in code — because the announcement says 'a package imports agentfront'. A declarative file (toml/yaml) is a possible secondary surface, parked
The single-source-of-truth idea survives the pivot: one registration of a function/doc drives BOTH its MCP tool exposure AND its HTTP doc page, so the two surfaces can't disagree

The CLI surface is no longer agentfront's headline output. agentfront keeps its OWN cli (learn/explain/doctor/serve) but generating a CLI FOR the host tool drops out of the v1 pivot scope (host gets MCP+HTTP from import; CLI-for-host is follow-up or retired)

HTTP: importing agentfront and pointing it at a directory of markdown yields an HTTP server that serves each .md as a page plus an auto-generated sitemap, with zero HTTP/server code written by the author

honesty_conditions.text: An author serves a directory of markdown as a navigable site (pages + sitemap) without writing any HTTP/server code — proven by agentfront serving its own docs/ this way

claims.text: MCP: registering a plain Python function with agentfront exposes it as an MCP tool (name, description, schema derived from the signature/docstring), with zero MCP-protocol code written by the author

honesty_conditions.text: A plain Python function becomes a callable MCP tool by registration alone — proven by an MCP client listing and invoking it, no protocol code in the host

claims.text: SSOT: one code-first config object is the single place a doc-or-tool is declared; the MCP menu and the HTTP pages both DERIVE from it, so the two surfaces cannot list divergent sets; serving both is one call

honesty_conditions.text: There is exactly one declaration site per doc-or-tool; deleting an entry removes it from BOTH the MCP menu and the HTTP site; no second registry can drift

claims.text: agentfront becomes a runtime framework that host packages IMPORT (not a generator they cite). Policy reconciliation: importing agentfront is SANCTIONED within the AgentCulture org; importing dependencies from OUTSIDE the org remains avoided and needs explicit approval as an exception. This constrains agentfront's OWN dependency footprint (favor stdlib; any outside dep needs approval)

The 'cli cite' scaffolder and the manifest->three-surfaces GENERATION vision are RETIRED. The rubric/doctor discipline is preserved by reframing it onto the runtime: doctor audits the LIVE served surfaces (sitemap present, MCP menu under threshold, learn affordance), not emitted files
Host import yields ALL THREE agent-first surfaces — CLI (argparse with learn+doctor), MCP server, and HTTP markdown+sitemap site — every one derived from the single code-first config (SSOT)

honesty_conditions.text: A single config produces all three surfaces, and they agree: the CLI 'learn', the MCP tool list, and the HTTP sitemap enumerate the SAME set of docs/tools — proven by agentfront serving its own three surfaces from one config

claims.text: MCP surface is built on the official 'mcp' Python SDK — granted as the FIRST sanctioned outside-org dependency exception under c17. (HTTP impl stays parked but defaults toward stdlib, since no further outside dep was approved.)

open_vagueness.text: MCP transport: stdio vs streamable-HTTP/SSE (or both). Likely stdio-first, but undecided
HTTP server implementation: stdlib http.server (zero-dep, agent-first minimalism) vs starlette/uvicorn (richer, heavier). Dependency weight is itself an agent-first concern
Declarative config file (toml/yaml) as a secondary surface alongside the Python API
Whether learn/doctor affordances are themselves served over MCP and HTTP (doctor-on-every-surface pillar applied to the runtime)
Fate of the teken compatibility wrapper and the 'three surfaces from one manifest' generator code already in agentfront/cite/
